Statement: Five persons R, S, T, U, V lives in a

five-story building as the ground floor is

numbered 1 and above it, 2 and so on until the

topmost floor is numbered 5 but not necessarily

in the same order. Between R and V, how many

people reside?

I. Two persons live between R and T. Between T

and U, only one person resides; T does not

reside on the top floor.

II. T lives above R. One person live between S

and V. V lives above U.

Directions: The question below consists of Two

statements. You have to decide whether the

data provided in the statements are sufficient

to answer the question.

Six friends - Karthi, Keerthi, Kabilan, Kapil, Kavi

and Kumar are sitting in a circular table facing

centre. One person sits between Keerthi and

Kavi. Who sits second to the left of Karthi?

Statement 1: Kabilan sits second to the right of

Kumar, who sits to the immediate right of Kavi.

Two persons sit between Keerthi and Karthi

(either from right or left)

Statement 2: Kavi and Karthi are immediate

neighbours. Kabilan sits to the immediate right

of Keerthi. Keerthi and Karthi are not immediate

neighbours.

are sufficient to answer the question. Read all

the statements and give the answer.

Seven family members - Arjun, Samay, Kavish,

Milan, Revan, Nitin and Viraj visit the temple

from Monday to Sunday. Who among the

following visits on Monday?

Statement I:- Milan visits the temple before

Samay. Only one person visits the temple

between Milan and Samay. Milan does not visit

temple after Wednesday. Three persons visit

the temple between Kavish and Revan, who

visits before Kavish.

Statement II:- Three persons visit between Nitin

and Samay. The number of persons visit before

and after Milan is the same. Only one person

visits between Nitin and Milan, who visits after

Nitin. Viraj does not visit to temple on Sunday.

Statement III:- Three persons visit the temple

between Arjun and Viraj, who visits after Arjun.

The number of persons visit temple after Samay

is same as the number of persons visit temple

before Nitin.

How many persons were born after O?

I. Number of persons born before Q is equal to

younger not just older than Q. P is younger than

Q but older than M.

II. Two persons were born between M and Q.

than Q but older than P.

older nor just younger to P. O is older than P.
